text:
Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful method is to set SMART goals: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve sales," a SMART goal might be "increase sales by 15% in six months."

You can break larger goals into smaller milestones. These are checkpoints that help you track progress. Each milestone should have clear deliverables, which are the actual results or products you create, like a report or a prototype.

Finally, make sure all stakeholders, such as team members and clients, agree on the goals early on. This alignment prevents confusion and keeps everyone working in the same direction.
